headline: GOLD HOLDS $4,878 INTO EASTER WEEKEND — GOLDMAN STANDS FIRM ON $5,400 YEAR-END TARGET
snippet: Gold settled at $4,878/oz on April 17 — the last session before the Easter holiday — extending a fourth consecutive week of gains. Central banks are buying 60 tonnes/month and Western ETFs have added ~500 tonnes since early 2025. Goldman Sachs holds its $5,400 year-end target after reaffirming the structural bull case following March's sharp 10% correction, the metal's steepest monthly drop since 2013.

headline: TSX-V SURGES 4.94% FOR WEEK: TINCORP +128%, Q-GOLD +103%, KLDC +93% AS JUNIOR GOLD ROARS
snippet: The S&P/TSX Venture Composite Index gained 4.94% in the week ended April 17 as junior miners surged on gold momentum. Tincorp Metals (TIN.V) led with a 128% weekly gain after releasing a 697,000-oz gold-copper resource update in Ecuador. Q-Gold Resources (QGR.V) jumped 103% on its maiden Quartz Mountain PEA (after-tax NPV US$1.71B at a US$3,265/oz base price — well below current spot). Kirkland Lake Discoveries (KLDC) rose 93% on a 103m gold intercept in its first 2026 drill hole. BC's 2025 mineral exploration investment hit a record C$751M, with copper surpassing gold for the first time.

headline: MAJORS CAN'T SOLVE THE RESERVE CRISIS ALONE — STRUCTURAL CASE FOR JUNIOR MINERS NEVER STRONGER
snippet: The world's biggest gold miners are depleting reserves faster than they can discover replacements — a structural problem that no amount of M&A spending can fix at 2026 valuations. The analysis argues that quality junior explorers represent the only realistic source of new mine supply over the next decade, making the sector compelling for investors who can tolerate development risk. Record margins and compressed multiples mean the timing has rarely looked better.

headline: KLDC HITS 103M CONTINUOUS GOLD IN FIRST DRILL HOLE AT ONTARIO'S MIRADO: 5.66 G/T OVER 18.2M
snippet: Kirkland Lake Discoveries (TSXV: KLDC) returned 103 metres of continuous gold mineralization in the first drill hole at Mirado, 20km southeast of Kirkland Lake, Ontario. Key intercepts include 5.66 g/t Au over 18.2m, 3.30 g/t over 18.7m, and 1.78 g/t over 23.9m. The results confirm the South Zone as a broad gold system — originally identified in the 1980s — with meaningful expansion potential. Stock surged 93% for the week on the back of this single hole.

headline: USA RARE EARTH (USAR) SURGES 7% ON NEW CCO HIRE AND US SUPPLY-CHAIN DEAL; OKLAHOMA MAGNET PLANT COMMISSIONED
snippet: USA Rare Earth (NYSE: USAR) rose 7.11% on April 17 after appointing Chaitan Kansal as Chief Commercial Officer and confirming a pivotal U.S. supply-chain partnership. Phase 1a of the company's Stillwater, Oklahoma NdFeB sintered magnet line is now commissioned, targeting 600 t/yr by end-2026 and 1,200 t/yr by early 2027. USAR still trades ~45% below its 52-week high despite being the only U.S. company building a fully integrated mine-to-magnet rare earth supply chain. New DOD rules restricting Chinese-sourced magnets from defence contracts kick in 2027.

headline: COT REPORT: GOLD SPECS STILL VASTLY UNDERPOSITIONED — DRY POWDER COULD TURBOCHARGE NEXT MOVE HIGHER
snippet: The April 17 Commitments of Traders report, published on GoldSeek, shows super-leveraged gold speculators positioned well below historical highs — leaving substantial dry powder for fresh buying that could amplify gold's next leg higher if sentiment shifts. Silver positioning shows similar room for expansion as industrial demand from solar manufacturing and energy storage keeps physical inventory tight. China imported 790+ tonnes of silver in early 2026, driven partly by solar production.

headline: WEST RED LAKE GOLD: 30.59 G/T OVER 1.0M AT ROWAN PROJECT — COMBINED MADSEN-ROWAN PFS TARGETED Q3 2026
snippet: West Red Lake Gold Mines (TSXV: WRLG) released infill drilling results from the Rowan Project in Ontario's Red Lake Mining District, including 1.0m at 30.59 g/t Au on Vein 001 and 1.62m at 19.83 g/t Au on Vein 013. The 37-hole, ~6,300m infill program is designed to support a combined Madsen-Rowan pre-feasibility study targeting Q3 2026. Results confirm and extend high-grade vein zones beyond the current PEA mine plan, with potential to increase daily mined tonnage.

headline: COPPER NEAR $6/LB AT 2-MONTH HIGH ON US-IRAN OPTIMISM; COPX COPPER MINERS ETF UP 145% YEAR-OVER-YEAR
snippet: Copper climbed toward $6.07/lb, fully recovering losses since the Middle East conflict began, as US-Iran ceasefire talks opened the Strait of Hormuz and lifted risk appetite across industrial metals. The Global X Copper Miners ETF (COPX) traded at $87.57 on April 17 with $7.76B in assets — up 145% total return over the past year. Structural tailwinds remain: AI data centre buildout, global defence spending, a Chilean government push to accelerate output, and First Quantum's $4.2B IFC-backed Taca Taca project in Argentina.

headline: CHINA SULFURIC ACID EXPORT BAN ADDS FRESH SHOCK TO SILVER MARKET ALREADY IN SIXTH-YEAR SUPPLY DEFICIT
snippet: China is halting sulfuric acid exports through the rest of 2026, creating a new supply shock for silver. Approximately 70% of annual silver output is a byproduct of copper mining — which requires sulfuric acid — meaning the ban directly threatens mine supply at a critical time. Silver demand is forecast to outstrip supply for the sixth consecutive year in 2026, with last year's deficit estimated at 95 million ounces. Physical investment offtake is forecast to rise 20% this year, and the cumulative 5-year deficit is approaching 800 million ounces — an entire year of global mining production.

headline: G MINING ACQUIRES G2 GOLDFIELDS FOR $2.2B TO BUILD 500,000-OZ/YR LOW-COST GUYANA GOLD HUB
snippet: G Mining Ventures (TSX: GMIN) agreed to acquire G2 Goldfields (TSX: GTWO) in an all-share deal valued at approximately C$3B (US$2.2B), merging adjacent Oko West and Oko-Ghanie gold projects in Guyana into a district-scale complex targeting 500,000+ oz/yr gold production. G2 shareholders receive a 72% premium to 30-day VWAP. The combined entity unlocks over C$1B in synergies from shared infrastructure and mine sequencing. Deal close expected Q2 2026; post-merger, GMIN shareholders retain ~80% of the combined entity.
